payload.message:
Run this command: sh ~/.openclaw/workspace-security/scripts/audit-lulu.sh

Read the output carefully. The script already gathers process details, reverse DNS, and whois data for unknown IPs.

IMPORTANT: If anything looks suspicious, YOU MUST INVESTIGATE IT YOURSELF before reporting. Do NOT recommend that Nelson investigate manually. Use exec to run commands like:
- ps aux | grep <process_or_pid> — to identify unknown processes
- lsof -p <pid> -i -n -P — to see what a process is doing
- lsof -i :<port> — to identify what's listening on a port
- whois <ip> — to look up IP ownership
- host <ip> — for reverse DNS
- codesign -dv --verbose=2 <binary_path> — to verify if a binary is signed by Apple/known vendor

Only report findings AFTER you have investigated them. Your report should include your conclusions, not open questions.

Also read ~/.openclaw/workspace-security/KNOWN_IPS.md — if an IP or port is already listed there, skip it. If you investigate something new and confirm it safe, append it to KNOWN_IPS.md using the edit tool.

DO NOT call any other write tools. NEVER say HEARTBEAT_OK.

KNOWN SAFE TRAFFIC (ignore these):
- Google/Chrome on ports 443, 5228
- Discord on port 443 to 162.159.x.x
- Telegram on port 443 to 149.154.x.x
- node (OpenClaw) on port 443
- Apple services (17.x.x.x) on ports 443, 5223
- Ollama, LuLu, mDNSResponder, trustd, ocspd, apsd, cloudd
- localhost connections (127.0.0.1)
- Anything already in KNOWN_IPS.md

FLAG AS SUSPICIOUS (after investigation):
- Unknown processes making outbound connections that you cannot identify
- Connections to unusual ports with no legitimate explanation
- Unsigned binaries with network access
- Connections to Tor exit nodes or known-bad IPs

ALWAYS reply in this exact format:

Security Audit Report — <today's date>

New outbound IPs detected:
- (list any non-standard IPs with process, port, and your finding — e.g. 'Confirmed: Anthropic API server' — or 'none')

New IPs added to known-safe:
- (list any IPs you added to KNOWN_IPS.md, or 'none')

Suspicious activity:
- (describe anything you could NOT explain after investigation, or 'none')

Risk level: None/Low/Medium/High/Critical

Actions taken:
- (what you investigated and concluded, or 'routine scan — all clear')

jobs.name: Daily OpenAI Codex rate limit monitor

payload.message:
Check for OpenAI Codex rate limit errors in the past 24 hours and take proactive action if needed.

1. Check logs for rate limit errors: grep -i 'API rate limit reached\|429\|quota' /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log | tail -20
2. Check auth profile for failures: cat /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json | jq '.usageStats."openai-codex:default"'
3. If there are more than 3 rate limit errors in 24h, or lastFailureAt is recent (<24h), consider:
   - Switching WhatsApp quick agent from GPT-5.4 to Ollama/GPT-OSS
   - Creating a report in memory/YYYY-MM-DD-rate-limit.md
   - Notifying via systemEvent if needed
4. Always be proactive - fix issues before Nelson reports them.

Reply with your findings and any actions taken.

You are the email triage agent for CuizAI (support@cuiz-ai.com). Check the inbox and classify emails.

Step 1: Check unread emails
```
exec command:"/Users/openclaw/.openclaw/email-pipeline/bin/zohomail unread 10"
```

Step 2: Classify each email into one of these categories:

SKIP (do NOT write a task file):
- Automated notifications (X/Twitter login alerts, password changes, 2FA codes)
- Marketing, newsletters, promotional emails
- Spam or phishing
- System alerts that don't need human action
- Transactional receipts, subscription confirmations

ACTIONABLE (write a task file with the correct category):
- "bug_report" — User reports something broken, not working, or behaving unexpectedly
- "feature_request" — User asks for new functionality or improvements
- "support_question" — User needs help using the platform, has a how-to question
- "business_inquiry" — Partnership, collaboration, sales, or business proposal
- "escalation" — Anything urgent, legal, complaint, or that clearly needs Nelson personally

Step 3: For each ACTIONABLE email, read the full content:
```
exec command:"/Users/openclaw/.openclaw/email-pipeline/bin/zohomail read <folderId> <messageId>"
```

Step 4: Write a task file to /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/email-pipeline/tasks/

Filename: YYYY-MM-DD_HHmm_<short-slug>.json

Format:
```json
{
  "created": "ISO-8601 timestamp",
  "source": "email",
  "category": "bug_report | feature_request | support_question | business_inquiry | escalation",
  "from": "sender@example.com",
  "sender_name": "Name",
  "subject": "Email subject",
  "message_id": "zoho_message_id",
  "folder_id": "zoho_folder_id",
  "summary": "Brief 1-2 sentence summary of what the user wants",
  "priority": "low | medium | high",
  "requires_code_change": true/false,
  "full_content": "The full email text"
}
```

Classification guide:
- requires_code_change: true for bug_report, feature_request, and anything that could involve updating files in the codebase (including docs, UI text, config). false ONLY for pure business_inquiry and escalation.
- priority: high if user is frustrated, paying customer, or blocking issue. medium for normal requests. low for nice-to-haves.

Step 5: For ALL emails (both actionable and skipped), mark them as processed (this also marks as read in Zoho):
```
exec command:"/Users/openclaw/.openclaw/email-pipeline/bin/zohomail mark-processed <messageId>"
```

Step 6: If no actionable emails, reply HEARTBEAT_OK.
If you wrote task files, reply with a brief count: e.g. "2 tasks: 1 bug_report (high), 1 feature_request (medium)"

Check for GCP alert task files:

exec command:"ls /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/email-pipeline/cuiz-ai-alerts/ 2>/dev/null"

Read any JSON files in that directory. If the directory is empty or doesn't exist, reply with exactly HEARTBEAT_OK.

For each alert task file found:

1. Read the task file to understand the alert
2. You MUST use the sessions_spawn tool with runtime set to "acp" and agentId set to "claude". This is MANDATORY — do NOT use any other spawn mode. Do NOT investigate the code yourself. Your ONLY job is to spawn Claude Code via ACP.

   Call sessions_spawn with these EXACT parameters:
   - runtime: "acp"
   - agentId: "claude"
   - mode: "session"
   - thread: true
   - cwd: "/Users/openclaw/projects/cuiz-ai"
   - streamTo: "parent"
   - task: "A GCP alert was triggered for CuizAI. Alert details: <paste alert summary and full_content from task file>.

     Steps:
     1. Run `gcloud logging read 'severity>=ERROR AND resource.type=cloud_run_revision' --project=quiz-ai-455111 --limit 50 --format json --freshness=2h` to get recent errors
     2. Analyze the errors and trace them to source code
     3. If the error is in application code: find the bug, fix it, run tests, commit, and create a PR with a clear title and description
     4. If the error is infrastructure/config: document what you found and recommend actions
     5. Write a result file to /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/email-pipeline/results/ with this structure:
        {\"created\": \"<ISO-8601>\", \"source\": \"gcp-alert\", \"project\": \"cuiz-ai\", \"alert_subject\": \"<subject>\", \"diagnosis\": \"<what you found>\", \"action_taken\": \"fix_and_pr\" | \"no_action\" | \"needs_manual\", \"pr_url\": \"<url or null>\", \"summary\": \"<human-readable summary>\"}
     6. When done run: openclaw system event --text 'CuizAI alert handled: <one-line summary>' --mode now"

3. After spawning, move the task file to /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/email-pipeline/archive/

jobs.name: Keep gnelson85 and nensol85 tokens alive
jobs.description: Weekly dummy gmail calls to keep both gog OAuth tokens alive

payload.message:
Run these commands to keep both gog tokens alive:

exec command:"gog gmail search 'newer_than:1d' -a gnelson85@gmail.com --max 1 2>&1"
exec command:"gog gmail search 'newer_than:1d' -a nensol85@gmail.com --max 1 2>&1"

If either returns an error about invalid_grant or token revoked, report which account's token has expired and that Nelson needs to re-authenticate.

jobs.name: proddy-product-report
jobs.description: Weekly CuizAI product analysis: query PostHog, take actions, write task files for coder

payload.message:
You are CuizAI's autonomous product manager. Analyze PostHog data, take direct actions where you can, and write task files for code changes the coder agent should make.

## PHASE 1: DATA COLLECTION

Query PostHog MCP for these metrics:

1. NORTH STAR — Weekly returning quiz generators:
   Use posthog insight-get (id: 3819193). Get latest week + WoW change.

2. ACTIVATION — Signup→first quiz (7d):
   Use posthog insight-get (id: 3819192). Get conversion rate + trend.

3. ACTIVATED USERS — Signup + completed + 2nd quiz within 7d:
   Use posthog insight-get (id: 3819249). Get count by signup week.

4. WEEKLY FUNNEL:
   Query for: unique visitors (pageview), signups (user_signed_up), uploads (quiz_generated), quizzes completed (quiz_completed). Report each step with conversion rates.

5. SHARE FEATURE — quiz_share_link_created and quiz_high_score_share_nudge_clicked events (7d).

6. ANON CONVERSION — anon_quota_signup_modal_shown events (7d), broken down by source.

7. SURVEY FEEDBACK — Post-Quiz Feedback survey (id: 0199fc6b-a1c5-0000-a512-19c96504ca48):
   a) Use posthog survey-stats (id: 0199fc6b-a1c5-0000-a512-19c96504ca48) for shown/dismissed/sent counts and conversion rate over the past 7 days.
   b) Use posthog query-run with HogQL to get actual responses from the past 7 days:
      - Average quality rating (Q1, scale 1-10)
      - Difficulty distribution (Q2: Too easy / Just right / Too hard)
      - 'What would make you come back' distribution (Q3: Better question quality / More quiz features / Faster generation / Different question types)
      - All open text feedback (Q4) — quote each response verbatim
   The survey events are 'survey sent' with properties $survey_id and $survey_response. Query for events where $survey_id = '0199fc6b-a1c5-0000-a512-19c96504ca48'.

## PHASE 2: ANALYSIS & ACTIONS

After collecting data, analyze for actionable insights.

Direct actions (do these yourself):
- If a PostHog insight is broken or returning no data, try to fix it via posthog insight-update
- If you spot a new event pattern worth tracking, create a new insight via posthog insight-create-from-query

Write task files when ANY of these triggers fire:
- Funnel drop-off > 20% WoW at any step → task to investigate and fix the UI step
- Activation rate drops below 15% → task to improve onboarding flow
- Share feature usage < 5 events/week → task to make share CTA more prominent in quiz results
- Anon conversion modal shown but conversion < 10% → task to improve modal copy/design
- Any metric shows 3+ weeks of continuous decline → task with analysis and proposed fix
- New pattern spotted (e.g., users drop off at a specific quiz length) → task to investigate
- Average quality rating drops below 7.0 → task to investigate quiz generation quality (check prompts, model, parsing)
- 'Too hard' or 'Too easy' exceeds 40% of responses → task to adjust difficulty defaults or add difficulty selector prominence
- One 'what would make you come back' option gets >50% of votes → task to prioritize that feature
- Open text feedback mentions a specific bug or repeated complaint → task to investigate and fix

Task file format — write to /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/proddy-tasks/YYYY-MM-DD_HHmm_<slug>.json:
{
  "created": "ISO-8601",
  "source": "proddy-product",
  "category": "funnel_optimization | onboarding_fix | ui_improvement | feature_experiment | bug_investigation",
  "priority": "low | medium | high",
  "title": "Short actionable title",
  "analysis": "What the data shows — include actual numbers and trends",
  "proposed_action": "Specific change to make. Be concrete: which component, what to change, why",
  "project": "cuiz-ai",
  "files_likely_affected": ["relative/path/from/project/root"],
  "acceptance_criteria": "How to verify the fix worked",
  "data_snapshot": { "metric": "value", "wow_change": "+/-N%" }
}

Priority: high = metric dropped >30% WoW or activation broken; medium = noticeable decline; low = optimization opportunity.

## PHASE 3: REPORT

Send a Telegram report with sections, numbers, WoW deltas (↑↓→).

Include a SURVEY section with: response rate, avg quality rating, difficulty breakdown, top 'come back' reason, and any notable open text quotes.

End with:
📋 Tasks created: N (list titles)
✅ Direct actions: N (list what you did)
⚡ No action needed (if everything healthy)

jobs.name: proddy-seo-report
jobs.description: Weekly CuizAI SEO analysis: query GSC, identify opportunities, write task files for coder

payload.message:
You are CuizAI's autonomous SEO manager. Analyze GSC data, identify issues and opportunities, and write task files for code changes the coder should make. The site is cuiz-ai.com.

## PHASE 1: DATA COLLECTION

First, call gsc list_properties to find the exact site_url.

Then query:

1. PERFORMANCE — gsc get_performance_overview (days=7) + gsc compare_search_periods (WoW). Get clicks, impressions, CTR, position + deltas.

2. TOP QUERIES (30) — gsc get_search_analytics dimensions='query', days=28, row_limit=30.

3. TOP PAGES (30) — gsc get_search_analytics dimensions='page', days=28, row_limit=30.

4. INDEXING HEALTH — gsc check_indexing_issues on: /, /upload, /sample-quiz, /sample-flashcards, /pricing, /blog, /sample/biology-quiz, /sample/history-quiz, /sample/ai-quiz, /sample/psychology-quiz, /sample/spanish-vocab-flashcards.

5. RICH RESULTS — gsc inspect_url_enhanced on / and /sample-quiz.

## PHASE 2: ANALYSIS & ACTIONS

Apply these rules to write task files to /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/proddy-tasks/:

### Opportunity queries → Meta optimization tasks
Find queries with: impressions > 10, CTR < 5%, position 5-20. For EACH opportunity (up to 5 per week), write a task file telling the coder to update the matching page's <title> and meta description to better target the query. Include the exact query, current ranking, and suggested title/description.

### Indexing issues → Fix tasks
If any key URL is not indexed or has crawl errors, write a task file with the specific issue and fix.

### Structured data errors → Fix tasks
If rich results check shows errors, write a task file naming the exact error and which component to fix (StructuredData.tsx or page layout).

### Content gap detection
Look at queries landing on homepage or generic pages that suggest demand for topics we don't have a dedicated /sample/ page for (e.g., 'chemistry quiz', 'math flashcards'). Write a LOW priority task to create a new subject page — include the query volume evidence.

### Blog canary
From top pages (row_limit=50), find /blog/ URLs getting impressions. If a blog post has impressions > 50 but CTR < 3%, write a task to optimize its title/meta.

### Direct actions:
- If a URL should be indexed but isn't, try gsc submit_sitemap or log it for manual review
- NEVER make code changes yourself — always write task files

Task file format — write to /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/proddy-tasks/YYYY-MM-DD_HHmm_<slug>.json:
{
  "created": "ISO-8601",
  "source": "proddy-seo",
  "category": "meta_optimization | structured_data_fix | indexing_fix | new_content | redirect_fix | blog_optimization",
  "priority": "low | medium | high",
  "title": "Short actionable title",
  "analysis": "What GSC data shows — include numbers",
  "proposed_action": "Specific change: e.g. 'Update title of /sample/biology-quiz from X to Y to target query Z'",
  "project": "cuiz-ai",
  "target_url": "https://cuiz-ai.com/path",
  "target_queries": ["query1", "query2"],
  "files_likely_affected": ["app/sample/[slug]/subjects.ts"],
  "acceptance_criteria": "Position for query X should improve from Y over 2-4 weeks",
  "data_snapshot": { "query": "...", "clicks": 0, "impressions": 0, "ctr": "0%", "position": 0 }
}

Priority: high = key page not indexed or structured data error on homepage; medium = opportunity query with good impressions; low = content gap or minor optimization.

## PHASE 3: REPORT

Send Telegram report with sections, WoW deltas (↑↓→).

End with:
📋 Tasks created: N (list titles)
✅ Direct actions: N (list what you did)
⚡ No action needed (if SEO is healthy)

state.lastError: GrammyError: Call to 'sendMessage' failed! (400: Bad Request: message is too long)

jobs.name: proddy-task-executor
jobs.description: Polls proddy-tasks/ and spawns Claude Code via ACP to execute each task autonomously

payload.message:
Check for Proddy task files:

exec command:"ls /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/proddy-tasks/*.json 2>/dev/null"

If no JSON files exist, reply with exactly HEARTBEAT_OK.

For each task file found, read it and process it:

1. Read the task JSON to understand what needs to be done
2. You MUST use sessions_spawn with runtime "acp" and agentId "claude". Do NOT investigate or write code yourself.

   Call sessions_spawn with these EXACT parameters:
   - runtime: "acp"
   - agentId: "claude"
   - mode: "session"
   - thread: true
   - cwd: "/Users/openclaw/projects/cuiz-ai/quiz-ai-generator-frontend"
   - streamTo: "parent"
   - task: Build the task description from the JSON fields. Include:
     a) The full analysis and proposed_action from the task file
     b) The files_likely_affected as starting points
     c) The acceptance_criteria as the definition of done
     d) These instructions:
        - First explore the codebase and understand the current state
        - Make the changes described in proposed_action
        - Run the dev server and verify the change works (next dev)
        - Run type checking (npx tsc --noEmit) to ensure no errors
        - Commit with a clear message referencing the data-driven reason
        - Create a PR with title and description explaining the change and the data behind it
        - When done run: openclaw system event --text 'Proddy task done: <title from task file>' --mode now

3. After spawning Claude Code, move the task file to archive:
   exec command:"mv /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/proddy-tasks/<filename>.json /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/proddy-tasks/archive/"

4. If multiple task files exist, process them ONE AT A TIME (spawn, wait for completion, then next). Do NOT spawn multiple sessions in parallel.

5. After all tasks are processed, report: "Executed N proddy tasks: <list titles>"

jobs.name: Memory Dreaming Promotion
jobs.description: [managed-by=memory-core.short-term-promotion] Promote weighted short-term recalls into MEMORY.md (limit=10, minScore=0.800, minRecallCount=3, minUniqueQueries=3, recencyHalfLifeDays=14, maxAgeDays=30).
